lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ਮਨਿ ਵੇਖਹੁ ਕੋ ਪਤੀਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur kinai na paaeo man vekhahu ko pateeae |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, no one finds the Lord; anyone can try and see.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਭੇਟੈ ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har kirapaa te satigur paaeeai bhettai sehaj subhaae |
lines.english: By the Lord's Grace, the True Guru is found, and then the Lord is met with intuitive ease.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਇਆ ਬਿਨੁ ਭਾਗਾ ਹਰਿ ਧਨੁ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: manamukh bharam bhulaaeaa bin bhaagaa har dhan na paae |5|
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh is deluded by doubt; without good destiny, the Lord's wealth is not obtained. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਨਹੁ ਪਾਇਨੑਿ ਮੋਖ ਦੁਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: mukat kade na hovee nahu paaeina mokh duaar |
lines.english: Those people are never liberated; they do not find the Door of Salvation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਸਭਾ ਧਾਤੁ ਹੈ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: trai gun sabhaa dhaat hai parr parr kareh veechaar |
lines.english: The three dispositions are completely distracting; people read and study and contemplate them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬੰਧਨ ਨ ਤੁਟਹੀ ਨਾਮਿ ਨ ਲਗੈ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: bin satigur bandhan na tuttahee naam na lagai piaar |6|
lines.english: Without the True Guru, they are never released from bondage; they do not embrace love for the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਮੋਨੀ ਥਕੇ ਬੇਦਾਂ ਕਾ ਅਭਿਆਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: parr parr panddit monee thake bedaan kaa abhiaas |
lines.english: The Pandits, the religious scholars, and the silent sages, reading and studying the Vedas, have grown weary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਚਿਤਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਨਹ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਵਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: har naam chit na aavee neh nij ghar hovai vaas |
lines.english: They do not even think of the Lord's Name; they do not dwell in the home of their own inner being.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮਕਾਲੁ ਸਿਰਹੁ ਨ ਉਤਰੈ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਕਪਟ ਵਿਣਾਸੁ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jamakaal sirahu na utarai antar kapatt vinaas |7|
lines.english: The Messenger of Death hovers over their heads; they are ruined by the deceit within themselves. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨਾਵੈ ਨੋ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਪਰਤਾਪਦਾ ਵਿਣੁ ਭਾਗਾਂ ਪਾਇਆ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har naavai no sabh ko parataapadaa vin bhaagaan paaeaa na jaae |
lines.english: Everyone longs for the Name of the Lord; without good destiny, it is not obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਗੁਰੁ ਭੇਟੀਐ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: nadar kare gur bhetteeai har naam vasai man aae |
lines.english: When the Lord bestows His Glance of Grace, the mortal meets the True Guru, and the Lord's Name comes to dwell within the mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੇ ਹੀ ਪਤਿ ਊਪਜੈ ਹਰਿ ਸਿਉ ਰਹਾਂ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੮॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak naame hee pat aoopajai har siau rahaan samaae |8|2|
lines.english: O Nanak, through the Name, honor wells up, and the mortal remains immersed in the Lord. ||8||2||
Malaar, Third Mehl, Ashtpadheeyaa, Second House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਕਾਰੈ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: har har kripaa kare gur kee kaarai laae |
lines.english: When the Lord shows His Mercy, He enjoins the mortal to work for the Guru.
True deliverance comes by focusing one's consciousness on the True Lord.
His pains are taken away, and the Lord's Name comes to dwell within.
Listen to the Shabad, and the Word of the Guru's Bani. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਮੇਰੇ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵਿ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: man mere har har sev nidhaan |
lines.english: O my mind, serve the Lord, Har, Har, the true treasure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਧਨੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਸਹਜਿ ਧਿਆਨੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur kirapaa te har dhan paaeeai anadin laagai sehaj dhiaan |1| rahaau |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, the wealth of the Lord is obtained. Night and day, focus your meditation on the Lord. ||1||Pause||
is ill-mannered and vile, wasted away into ruin.
The soul-bride who adorns herself without her Husband Lord,
Forgetting the Naam, the Name of the Lord, he performs all sorts of empty rituals. ||2||
This is the useless way of life of the self-willed manmukh.
The bride who is Gurmukh is beautifully embellished.
Through the Word of the Shabad, she enshrines her Husband Lord within her heart.
She realizes the One Lord, and subdues her ego.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਦਾਤੇ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur daate kinai na paaeaa |
lines.english: Without the Guru, the Giver, no one finds the Lord.
Only a few spiritual teachers realize this,
The greedy self-willed manmukh is attracted and engrossed in duality.
That soul-bride is virtuous and noble. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਹਿ ਕਹਣੁ ਕਹੈ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: keh keh kehan kahai sabh koe |
lines.english: Everyone tells the stories told by others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਭੇਟੇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: bin gur bhette mukat na hoe |4|
lines.english: that without meeting the Guru, liberation is not obtained. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਮਨ ਮੂਏ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin man mooe bhagat na hoe |
lines.english: Without subduing the mind, devotional worship does not come.
The Naam, the Name of the Lord, comes to abide in that heart. ||5||
In egotism, everyone can pretend to worship God with devotion.
When the intellect achieves spiritual wisdom, the heart-lotus blossoms forth.
By speaking and preaching, the mortal only shows off his self-conceit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਮਨੁ ਭੀਜੈ ਨਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: naa man bheejai naa sukh hoe |
lines.english: But this does not soften the mind, and it does not bring peace.
His devotional worship is useless, and his life is a total waste. ||6||
They alone are devotees, who are pleasing to the Mind of the True Guru.
They behold the Naam, the Name of the Lord, ever-present, near at hand.
Night and day, they remain lovingly attuned to the Name.
